mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where unemployment rises above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or returns are negative when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of high inflation.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not correlate with negative 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5%, liquidity conditions become exceptionally loose, compressing risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns are not significantly positive when rates are below 0.5%, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces negative returns in more than 55% of occurrences or fails to beat a baseline model in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly measures aggregate economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1% signals a sharp manufacturing slowdown that reduces corporate profit expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal fails to predict negative returns with a precision above 55% in walk-forward validation.
